speaker: WARWICK
text: What counsel, lords? Edward from Belgia,

speaker: WARWICK
text: With hasty Germans and blunt Hollanders,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Hath passed in safety through the Narrow Seas,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And with his troops doth march amain to London,

speaker: WARWICK
text: And many giddy people flock to him.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Let’s levy men and beat him back again.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: A little fire is quickly trodden out,

speaker: CLARENCE
text: Which, being suffered, rivers cannot quench.

speaker: WARWICK
text: In Warwickshire I have true-hearted friends,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Not mutinous in peace yet bold in war.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Those will I muster up; and thou, son Clarence,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Shalt stir up in Suffolk, Norfolk, and in Kent

speaker: WARWICK
text: The knights and gentlemen to come with thee.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: Thou, brother Montague, in Buckingham,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Northampton, and in Leicestershire shalt find

speaker: WARWICK
text: Men well inclined to hear what thou command’st.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: And thou, brave Oxford, wondrous well beloved,

speaker: WARWICK
text: In Oxfordshire shalt muster up thy friends.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: My sovereign, with the loving citizens,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Like to his island girt in with the ocean,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Or modest Dian circled with her nymphs,

speaker: WARWICK
text: Shall rest in London till we come to him.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Fair lords, take leave, and stand not to reply.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: Farewell, my sovereign.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Farewell, my Hector and my Troy’s true hope.

speaker: CLARENCE
text: In sign of truth, I kiss your Highness’ hand.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Well-minded Clarence, be thou fortunate.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: Comfort, my lord; and so I take my leave.

speaker: OXFORD
text: And thus I seal my truth, and bid adieu.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Sweet Oxford and my loving Montague

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And all at once, once more a happy farewell.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Farewell, sweet lords. Let’s meet at Coventry.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Here at the palace will I rest awhile.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Cousin of Exeter, what thinks your Lordship?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Methinks the power that Edward hath in field

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Should not be able to encounter mine.

speaker: EXETER
text: The doubt is that he will seduce the rest.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: That’s not my fear. My meed hath got me fame.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I have not stopped mine ears to their demands,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Nor posted off their suits with slow delays.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My pity hath been balm to heal their wounds,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My mildness hath allayed their swelling griefs,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: My mercy dried their water-flowing tears.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: I have not been desirous of their wealth

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Nor much oppressed them with great subsidies,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Nor forward of revenge, though they much erred.

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: Then why should they love Edward more than me?

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: No, Exeter, these graces challenge grace;

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: And when the lion fawns upon the lamb,

speaker:
KING
               
              HENRY
text: The lamb will never cease to follow him.

speaker: EXETER
text: Hark, hark, my lord, what shouts are these?

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Seize on the shamefaced Henry, bear him hence,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And once again proclaim us King of England.—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: You are the fount that makes small brooks to flow.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Now stops thy spring; my sea shall suck them dry

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And swell so much the higher by their ebb.—

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Hence with him to the Tower. Let him not speak.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: And, lords, towards Coventry bend we our course,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Where peremptory Warwick now remains.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: The sun shines hot, and if we use delay,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Cold biting winter mars our hoped-for hay.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Away betimes, before his forces join,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And take the great-grown traitor unawares.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Brave warriors, march amain towards Coventry.
